LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, WADE NOGY, COLLEEN MCKEOWN, DEBRA RUCKERT AND TERRI PILLOW NORIEGA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE VAGUE, UNCERTAIN, AND UNINTELLIGIBLE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SPECIFY FACTS THAT SUPPORT INDIVIDUAL CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST EACH DEFENDANT. INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS CANNOT BE HELD LIABLE FOR DISCRIMINATION AND RETALIATION CLAIMS UNDER FEHA. (SEE JONES V. TORREY PINES PARTNERSHIP (2008) 42 CAL.4TH 1158.) NO PUBLISHING TO SUPPORT DEFAMATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILTY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF HOLLY BRATT MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. COUNSEL GAVE NOTICE, PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.3., HOWEVER COUNSEL STATED THAT NOTICE WAS LATE BY TEN MINUTES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO HEAR THE MOTION BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT HAS BEEN DISMISSED AND A FINAL JUDGMENT ENTERED (CCP SECTION 581D; SAFECO INS. CO. VS. ARCHITECTURAL FACADES UNLIMITED, INC. (2005) 134 CAL.APP. 4TH 1477, 1482. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion For New Trial, For Order Vacating Judgment And For Relief Per Ccp Sec 662
Off Calendar Aug-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HOLLY BRATT Motion for New Trial, for Order Vacating Judgment and for Relief Per CCP Section 662. Appearances: John F. Mounier, Esq. of the Employee Rights Law Group 888-8361 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Christopher D. Baker, Esq. of Nixon Peabody LLP 984-8493 appearing for Defendants and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. The Court adds: Court bases its reasoning as follows: Court's prior ruling not erroneous; no showing of diligence why new facts not presented at an earlier time; failure to plead facts with enough specificity; and, CCP section 581(d) and CCP section 1008 apply. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2009 Off Calendar

Ntc And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Sustaining Demurrer To Pltf'Sthird Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend
Off Calendar Aug-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HOLLY BRATT Motion for Reconsideration of Order Sustaining Demurrer to Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint without Leave to Amend. Appearances: John Mounier, Esq. Employee Rights Law Group, (415) 888-8361, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party. Counsel gave notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3., however counsel stated that notice was late by ten minutes. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for reconsideration is denied. Court lacks jurisdiction to hear the motion because the complaint has been dismissed and a final judgment entered (CCP section 581d; Safeco Ins. Co. vs. Architectural Facades Unlimited, Inc. (2005) 134 Cal.App. 4th 1477, 1482. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Jun-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, Defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals, Wade Nogy, Colleen Mckeown, Debra Ruckert and Terri Noriega's motion for protective order, is taken off calendar, at the request of the moving party. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, WADE NOGY, COLLEEN MCKEOWN, DEBRA RUCKERT AND TERRI PILLOW NORIEGA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: John F. Mounier, Esq. of The Employee Rights Law Group 888-8361 for pltf & opposing party; Christopher D. Baker, Esq. of Nixon Peabody LLP 984-8493 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE VAGUE, UNCERTAIN, AND UNINTELLIGIBLE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SPECIFY FACTS THAT SUPPORT INDIVIDUAL CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST EACH DEFENDANT. INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS CANNOT BE HELD LIABLE FOR DISCRIMINATION AND RETALIATION CLAIMS UNDER FEHA. (SEE JONES V. TORREY PINES PARTNERSHIP (2008) 42 CAL.4TH 1158.) NO PUBLISHING TO SUPPORT DEFAMATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILTY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. Defendant to prepare proposed form of order. (302/CWW/JW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, WADE NOGY, COLLEEN MCKEOWN, DEBRA RUCKERT AND TERRI PILLOW NORIEGA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: John F. Mounier, Jr., Esq. of The Employee Rights Law Group 888-8361 for pltf & opposing party; Chris Baker, Esq. of Nixon Peabody 984-8493 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court sustained the demurrer with leave to amend by 12/31/08. This is the last time for plaintiff to amend the complaint, & any further late filed opposition will no longer be considered by the Court. Defendant to prepare proposed form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Pltf'S Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, WADE NOGY, COLLEEN MCKEOWN, DEBRA RUCKERT AND TERRI PILLOW NORIEGA'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Complaint.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained with 10 days leave to amend on each of the grounds asserted. Plaintiff must plead facts that enable each defendant to understand the factual and legal basis for claims alleged as to them. Each cause of action is to state only one legal claim. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
